WARNING: These test points are for A2485 (820-02100) ONLY. EVT schematic — component designators may differ from production. For A2442 (14" variant), use that board's diagnostic files.
data_source: Schematic analysis of J316-EVTs (155 pages). Adapted from A2442 diagnostic data (same X2728 MLB platform). No A2485-specific Saf repair data yet.

faults.name: PPBUS_AON cap short
faults.frequency: Common pattern (1 confirmed on A2442 sibling)
faults.root_cause: Capacitor short on PPBUS_AON distribution. Same topology as A2442.
faults.fix: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON decoupling caps near battery connector (page 27). Find short, remove cap, retest.

faults.name: CD3217 liquid damage (UF400/UF500/UG400)
faults.frequency: Common pattern (2 confirmed on A2442 sibling)
faults.root_cause: Liquid ingress under CD3217 or level shifter ICs. Corrodes BGA pads.
faults.fix: Clean board thoroughly. Replace affected CD3217. Check level shifters on pages 75, 82.
faults.note: A2485 uses UF000/UF100/UG000 designators (vs UF400/UF500/UG400 on A2442). Same IC, different designator.

faults.name: Viper VR (U9300) failure -- Pro/Max specific
faults.root_cause: Viper VR controller dead or power stage failure. Board partially boots but PCPU domains don't power up. 3 phase inputs on page 46 (PH1/PH2/PH3).
faults.fix: If VIPER_ALERT_L is low -> Viper has detected a fault. Check power stages (pages 48-49). Check PVDDDOM signals for PWM/EN status.
faults.note: Max variant has more phases than Pro -- more failure points

faults.name: Monaco VR failure -- Pro/Max specific
faults.root_cause: Monaco VR not starting. GPU/fabric domains not powered.
faults.fix: Check Monaco instances: C0 (page 50, UA100), C1 (page 51, UA200), C2/C4 (page 52, UA500). Check CLVR throttle on page 53.
faults.note: Monaco has 3+ instances. Failure of one instance may leave partial GPU function. UC000/UC001 on page 53 for throttle support.

faults.name: U5700 SVR AON failure
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all M-series boards
faults.root_cause: SVR AON controller dead, phase driver fault, or downstream short on PP3V8_AON pulling current.
faults.fix: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN. If enable asserted but no output -> U5700 dead. If output shorted -> thermal cam on PP3V8_AON branches to SPMU/MPMU/WiFi/TCON.

faults.name: MPMU (U8100) failure
faults.affected_rail: All MPMU buck and LDO outputs
faults.root_cause: Dead MPMU, liquid damage on BGA, or downstream short loading outputs.
faults.test_points: PMU test points page 129, MPMU output caps (page 40)
faults.fix: If multiple outputs dead -> replace U8100. If single output dead -> find downstream short on that rail.

faults.name: Dual SSD / Ocarina fault
faults.root_cause: SSD0 or SSD1 NAND failure, Ocarina controller dead, or NAND power VR fault.
faults.fix: Check both SSD power rails independently. If one SSD's power is shorted -> isolate that SSD. Try DFU restore. If NAND VR shorted -> replace Ocarina.
faults.note: A2485 has dual SSD (SSD0 + SSD1, 8 NAND packages total). Failure of one SSD may still allow partial boot.

faults.name: HDMI Madea controller fault
faults.fix: Board boots normally. HDMI output dead. Check Madea power rails, HDMI DDC I2C (page 86), HPD signal.

faults.name: SD card controller fault
faults.fix: Check PP3V3_AWAKE_SW_SD (power switch). Check SD_CD_L (card detect). Check controller IC. SD connector 514-00175.

faults.name: Dual fan failure (A2485-specific)

symptoms.dcps_20v: Normal current but thermal shutdown / fan noise

faults.root_cause: Fan controller or connector failure. 16" has dual fans -- failure of one may cause asymmetric cooling and thermal throttle.
faults.fix: Check FAN_PWR_EN, FAN_PWR_EN_L. Check SMC_FAN_0_TACH and SMC_FAN_1_TACH for tach feedback. If one fan dead and other spinning -> connector or controller fault on dead side.
faults.note: A2485-specific: 16" has dual fans. A2442 (14") has single fan. This is a key difference.
